originalTitle: A trillion dollars is a stupid amount of money
summary: Elon Musk is now officially the world's first trillionaire. That is a colossal amount of wealth (and by proxy, power) for one individual to have. Its scale - a thousand times more than a billion - is difficult to fathom for those of us who aren't among the 3,363 billionaires that currently exist in our […]
content: Elon Musk is now officially the world’s first trillionaire. That is a colossal amount of wealth (and by proxy, power) for one individual to have. Its scale — a thousand times more than a billion — is difficult to fathom for those of us who aren’t among the 3,363 billionaires that currently exist in our world. But let’s try to comprehend it anyway. The most frequently cited comparison is time. If you were to count out a million seconds, it would take you 11 and a half days. A billion seconds would take you 31.7 years. But a trillion seconds would take 31,700 years — to reach that point today, you would have needed to start counting in the Paleolithic era, around the time that Neanderthals went extinct. How about distance? Let’s say you earn $1 million dollars for every meter you walk. If you started at Times Square in NYC, you could make $1 billion by just walking down the street to The Museum of Modern Art. But to reach a trillion dollars, you would have to walk 621 miles, which is about 23 consecutive marathons. According to Google Maps, that’s the equivalent of walking from Times Square to Dayton, Ohio, and it would take around nine and a half days to do so. [Media: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Pr8P-WOQdX0] You could also visualize by weight, as a US dollar bill weighs exactly one gram — a consistency that’s designed to make it easier to count stacks of money. A million $1 bills would therefore weigh a metric ton (2,204 pounds), which is roughly equivalent to a small hatchback car. A trillion $1 bills would weigh the same as 5,000 of the biggest blue whales the world has ever seen (they can reach 200 metric tons) piled on top of each other. The Wall Street Journal has a cool visualization if you want to see how this looks in pennies. A million pennies stacked would reach about a mile high, or almost four Empire State Buildings. But a stack of 1 trillion pennies would reach the Moon and back — twice. [Image: …Stonks to the Moon, I guess? https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/Screenshot-2026-06-12-120828.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] You get the idea. And that vast sum of money could do many, many things. A trillion dollars is more than enough to solve world hunger by 2030, according to the United Nations, which estimates it would take $93 billion per year. That leaves Musk with $628 billion — enough to also fund the $600 billion that OpenAI is expected to spend on compute by 2030, and he’d still have enough change to be richer than 99.9999 percent of the global population. If we split $1 trillion across the entire 349 million US population, then every person would get $2,865. Or, if you theoretically had the liquidity to put $1 trillion into a bank account with a standard 4 percent interest rate, you would earn roughly $110 million every day — almost enough to launch two Falcon 9 rockets. Just remember: The next time someone tells you to count your pennies, Elon Musk wouldn’t be able to do so in a lifetime, even if he recruited his small army of offspring to help.
chineseTitle: 一万亿美元是一笔愚蠢的钱
chineseSummary: 埃隆·马斯克现在正式成为世界上第一位万亿富翁。对于一个人来说，这是一笔巨大的财富（以及权力）。它的规模——比十亿多一千倍——对于我们这些不属于我们目前存在的 3,363 亿万富翁的人来说是很难理解的[…]
chineseContent: 埃隆·马斯克现在正式成为世界上第一位万亿富翁。对于一个人来说，这是一笔巨大的财富（以及权力）。对于我们这些不属于目前世界上 3,363 名亿万富翁的人来说，其规模——比 10 亿多一千倍——很难理解。但无论如何，让我们尝试去理解它。最常被引用的比较是时间。如果数一百万秒，则需要 11 天半。 10 亿秒需要 31.7 年。但一万亿秒需要 31,700 年——今天要达到这一点，你需要从旧石器时代开始计算，大约在尼安德特人灭绝的时候。距离怎么样？假设您每行走一米即可赚取 100 万美元。如果你从纽约时代广场开始，只要沿着街道走到现代艺术博物馆，你就可以赚到 10 亿美元。但要达到一万亿美元，你必须步行 621 英里，相当于连续 23 场马拉松。根据谷歌地图，这相当于从时代广场步行到俄亥俄州代顿，大约需要九天半的时间。 [媒体：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Pr8P-WOQdX0] 您还可以通过重量来想象，因为一张美元钞票的重量恰好是一克——这种一致性旨在让数钞票变得更容易。因此，100 万张 1 美元钞票的重量为 1 公吨（2,204 磅），大约相当于一辆小型掀背车。一万亿张 1 美元钞票的重量相当于 5000 头世界上最大的蓝鲸（重达 200 吨）叠在一起的重量。如果你想看看这在便士中是什么样子，《华尔街日报》有一个很酷的可视化。一百万便士叠起来会
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
